content.text: I found the relevant S3 controller copy path: `x-amz-copy-source` is parsed in `S3Controller.handleCopyObject`, while normal object keys arrive via decoded path params. I’ll add a temporary regression test that uses a percent-encoded multibyte `CopySource` header to reproduce the mismatch.

void copyObjectWithNonAsciiSourceKey() {
        String encodedSourceKey = "src/%E3%83%86%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88%E7%94%BB%E5%83%8F.png";
        String encodedDestKey = "dst/%E3%83%86%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88%E7%94%BB%E5%83%8F.png";

        given()
            .contentType("text/plain")
            .body("hello")
        .when()
            .put("/test-bucket/" + encodedSourceKey)
        .then()
            .statusCode(200);

        given()
        .when()
            .get("/test-bucket/" + encodedSourceKey)
        .then()
            .statusCode(200)
            .body(equalTo("hello"));

        given()
            .header("x-amz-copy-source", "/test-bucket/" + encodedSourceKey)
        .when()
            .put("/test-bucket/" + encodedDestKey)
        .then()
            .statusCode(200)
            .body(containsString("CopyObjectResult"));

        given()
        .when()
            .get("/test-bucket/" + encodedDestKey)
        .then()
            .statusCode(200)
            .body(equalTo("hello"));
    }

content.text:
Implemented the S3 `CopyObject` fix for non-ASCII/multibyte source keys.

What changed:
- Updated `S3Controller.handleCopyObject(...)` to percent-decode the `x-amz-copy-source` bucket/key before looking up the source object.
- Added a path-component decoder that:
  - Decodes UTF-8 percent-encoded byte sequences, including multibyte characters.
  - Does not treat `+` as a space, which is important for S3 key semantics.
  - Returns `InvalidArgument` for malformed percent-encoding.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test for copying `src/テスト画像.png`.
- Verified the test passes with:

```bash
./mvnw -Dmaven.compiler.release=21 -Dtest=S3IntegrationTest#copyObjectWithNonAsciiSourceKey test
```

Notes:
- Running without overriding `maven.compiler.release` failed because the local JDK is Java 21 while the project defaults to Java 25.
- The temporary regression test was removed afterward as requested.
- No commits were made.
- Existing unrelated working-tree modification remains in `src/main/java/io/github/hectorvent/floci/services/s3/S3Service.java`; I did not alter or revert it.

Implemented the S3 `CopyObject` fix for non-ASCII/multibyte source keys.

What changed:
- Updated `S3Controller.handleCopyObject(...)` to percent-decode the `x-amz-copy-source` bucket/key before looking up the source object.
- Added a path-component decoder that:
  - Decodes UTF-8 percent-encoded byte sequences, including multibyte characters.
  - Does not treat `+` as a space, which is important for S3 key semantics.
  - Returns `InvalidArgument` for malformed percent-encoding.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test for copying `src/テスト画像.png`.
- Verified the test passes with:

```bash
./mvnw -Dmaven.compiler.release=21 -Dtest=S3IntegrationTest#copyObjectWithNonAsciiSourceKey test
```

Notes:
- Running without overriding `maven.compiler.release` failed because the local JDK is Java 21 while the project defaults to Java 25.
- The temporary regression test was removed afterward as requested.
- No commits were made.
- Existing unrelated working-tree modification remains in `src/main/java/io/github/hectorvent/floci/services/s3/S3Service.java`; I did not alter or revert it.
